message:
refactor(config): expose open_storage_with_startup_config so sync can avoid double-loading the startup snapshot

Sync's `execute()` was loading the startup config snapshot twice:
once at the top (as `startup`, used to compute `path_policy` before
the open-storage call after commit 90105c8), and again inside
`open_storage_with_cli_impl` itself (which re-read the beads config
family from disk to produce its own `StartupConfig`).

Two disk reads, two Layer merges, two implicit-external-JSONL
probes per invocation — all producing byte-identical state. Not a
correctness bug, but wasted work on every sync call, and the
doubled I/O showed up in strace under `br sync --status` (the
hottest non-write path).

Refactor:
  - Hoists `open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy` + all the
    layer / lock-timeout / auto_rebuilt plumbing into a new
    `pub(crate) open_storage_with_startup_config(startup:
    StartupConfig, cli: &CliOverrides, defer_jsonl_recovery: bool)`.
  - `open_storage_with_cli_impl` becomes a thin adapter that calls
    `load_startup_config_with_paths` and delegates to the new
    function.
  - `execute()` in sync.rs now loads the startup snapshot once and
    calls `config::open_storage_with_startup_config(startup, cli,
    defer_jsonl_recovery)` directly.

No observable behavior change. The `beads_dir` value threaded
through the new function is pulled from `startup.paths.beads_dir`
rather than re-passed by the caller, so the single source of
truth stays with the StartupConfig struct.

Cargo.lock intentionally not committed — local path-override
artifact.
